summary: Children from overindulgent homes are likely to display aggressive behavior due to a lack of structured guidance early-on leading potentially towards Oppositional Defiant Disorder later life. Such upbringing also hinders their ability to set appropriate limits resulting in difficulty integrating socially as they matured through adolescence into adult years where conflicts with authority figures become common issues requiring intervention management tailored specifically addressing underlying psychological factors influencing conduct patterns positively impacting overall well-being
